title: The Reset Gate
concept: The reset gate determines how much of the past hidden state to forget when calculating the new candidate state, allowing the model to discard irrelevant context.

exercises.prompt: Which gate in a GRU decides how much of the past hidden state to ignore? the GRU uses to decide how much of the past memory to discard when creating a new candidate state should ignore the past?
exercises.explain: In GRUs, the Reset Gate is the specific component responsible for determining how much of the past information is allowed to flow forward when proposing a new candidate state.

cards.title: Clear the Board!
cards.body: When reading, do you keep every single detail about the previous sentence in your head, or do you clean your mental whiteboard when the topic changes? In GRU networks, we have a specialized gate just for cleaning that whiteboard!

cards.title: What is the Reset Gate?
cards.body: The Reset Gate is a control valve in a Gated Recurrent Unit (GRU). It decides exactly how much of the past memory (hidden state) to ignore when building a brand new candidate state. If it's set to zero, the network completely forgets the past and starts fresh.

cards.title: The Detective's Notepad
cards.body: Imagine you are reading a long mystery novel. When you start a brand new chapter with a completely new set of characters in a different city, you temporarily put aside what the old characters were doing. The Reset Gate acts like your brain deciding to "ignore the old setting for a moment" so you can focus entirely on the new clues.

cards.title: Context Switching
cards.body: If a model is processing the sentence: "The dog barked. Meanwhile, at the space station...", the Reset Gate will close (output near 0) when transitioning from the dog to the space station, helping the model ignore the dog context while writing the new space-related memories.

cards.title: Temporary Block, Not Permanent Loss
cards.body: Many believe the Reset Gate deletes memories permanently from the model's main memory. Actually, it only blocks the past state *temporarily* while calculating the current candidate state. The main update gate will still decide what ultimately stays or goes in the long run.

cards.title: Open vs. Closed Reset Gate
cards.body: Depending on the reset gate's value, the GRU either holds onto the past context or wipes it clean to focus on what's happening right now.

right.text: Discards the past context to start fresh on a new sentence or topic.

left.text: Keeps the old context active to help understand continuous, connected sentences.

exercises.prompt: What is the main job of the Reset Gate in a GRU?

options.text: It decides how much of the final output to print on the screen.
It controls how much past memory to mix with new input for the candidate state.
It resets the weights of the network to random numbers during training.
It shuts off the power to the computer if the model makes a mistake.

exercises.explain: The Reset Gate decides how much of the previous hidden state (past memory) is combined with the current input to form the new candidate state.

exercises.prompt: True or False: A reset gate value close to 0 means the model will ignore most of the past hidden state when making the new candidate state.
exercises.explain: When the reset gate is close to 0, it tells the unit to ignore the past state, effectively letting the model start with a clean slate for the current step.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the blank to describe the main purpose of the reset gate.
exercises.sentence: The reset gate decides how much past information to [blank] when calculating the new candidate state.
exercises.explain: The reset gate determines how much of the past hidden state to forget or ignore when building the temporary candidate state.

exercises.prompt: Which of these statements about the Reset Gate was written by a confused AI chatbot?

options.text: The reset gate outputs values between 0 and 1 using a sigmoid function.
The reset gate is a physical lever on the GPU that you pull to erase the database.
A lower reset gate value means the model relies less on the past context.

exercises.explain: AI generated text (Option B) is completely irrelevant and nonsensical in the context of neural network architectures, whereas options A and C correctly describe GRU gates.

exercises.prompt: If a text generator suddenly encounters a brand new topic after a period, how does the Reset Gate react to help write the candidate state?

options.text: It drops close to 0 to clear out the old context for the new topic.
It spikes to 1 to force the network to keep every single past detail.
It shuts down the update gate completely so no new words can enter.
It randomly shuffles all words in the sentence to confuse the model.

exercises.explain: When starting a new paragraph or topic, the Reset Gate drops to near zero to wipe away the old, irrelevant context, allowing the candidate state to focus entirely on the fresh incoming words.
